renalAdjustments.recommendation: A nonsteroidal mineralocorticoid receptor antagonist (ns-MRA) can be added to first-line therapy for patients with T2D and high residual risks of kidney disease progression and cardiovascular events, as evidenced by persistent albuminuria (>30 mg/g [>3 mg/mmol])

drugs.sourceSnippet: A nonsteroidal mineralocorticoid receptor antagonist (ns-MRA) can be added to first-line therapy for patients with T2D and high residual risks of kidney disease progression and cardiovascular events, as evidenced by persistent albuminuria (>30 mg/g [>3 mg/mmol])
